content:
🌟 fresh | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a powerful, fast, and user-friendly text editing experience directly within the terminal, suitable for developers who prefer a terminal-based workflow or need to handle large files efficiently.
✨ Key Features: • Zero configuration • Familiar keybindings • Mouse support • IDE-level features • Command Palette & Fuzzy Finder
📖 Summary: Fresh is a modern, full-featured text editor designed for the terminal, offering IDE-level features like multi-cursor editing, a command palette, and LSP support with zero configuration. It prioritizes performance, handling large files efficiently and providing a familiar user experience akin to graphical editors.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ intercept | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a comprehensive and integrated platform for signal intelligence (SIGINT) gathering, analysis, and decoding using various software-defined radio (SDR) and related tools.
✨ Key Features: • Pager Decoding (POCSAG/FLEX) • 433MHz Sensor Decoding (rtl_433) • Sub-GHz RF Analysis (HackRF) • Aircraft Tracking (ADS-B) • Vessel Tracking (AIS)
📖 Summary: iNTERCEPT is an open-source, web-based platform that integrates a wide array of signal intelligence tools into a unified interface. It supports various signal decoding, tracking, and analysis functionalities, making advanced SIGINT capabilities accessible.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 zen | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A self-hosted, private note-taking and knowledge management application.
✨ Key Features: • Single Go binary or Docker Compose • Low resource usage • Standard Markdown files, local SQLite database • Organize with flexible tags • Full-text search with BM25 ranking
📖 Summary: Zen is a self-hosted notes application that stores notes as Markdown files in a local SQLite database. It offers features like full-text search, flexible tagging, and data portability, all within a single, low-resource binary or Docker container.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 Jauth | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securing and managing access to self-hosted applications through a lightweight reverse proxy with flexible authorization methods.
✨ Key Features: • Single binary executable • SSL/TLS encryption (self-signed or Let's Encrypt) • User authorization via SSH or Telegram • Optional Single Sign-On • Whitelist-based access control
📖 Summary: Jauth is a Go-based, lightweight SSL/TLS reverse proxy designed to secure self-hosted applications. It provides authorization through SSH or Telegram and supports Single Sign-On (SSO), simplifying access management for internal services.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 GitJournal | Dart
🎯 Primary Use Case: A private and portable note-taking solution integrated with Git for version control and flexible hosting.
✨ Key Features: • Mobile-first note taking • Stores notes in Git repositories • Uses Markdown + YAML header format • Supports GitHub, GitLab, and custom Git providers • Focus on privacy and data portability
📖 Summary: GitJournal is a mobile-first note-taking application that prioritizes privacy and data portability by storing notes in a standardized Markdown format within a Git repository. Users can choose to self-host their notes or use various Git providers like GitHub and GitLab.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Monic | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Controlling external monitor settings (brightness, volume, input) directly from the system tray on Linux.
✨ Key Features: • Brightness control with smooth animation • Volume control for built-in monitor speakers • Input source switching • Multiple monitor support • System tray interface
📖 Summary: Monic is a Python-based utility for Linux that provides system tray control over external monitor settings like brightness, volume, and input source. It features smooth animations for brightness changes and supports multiple monitors with an automatic update mechanism.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
